title: Chapter Thirty-Five: The Desolate Ma Family
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The return journey was leisurely.

Ma Jin gave tacit approval. Radiant Demon Wing had more than earned it -- the Thieving Ghost dead, the Great Violet Pill recovered, the Blood Flame Sect's raiding force destroyed, and the Mount Hua Sect's head disciple saved. Cheong Myeong rode with his bright smile and his one bandaged eye. Baek Mi-ryeo shook her head at all of them. Hyeon Won-chang suggested every tavern they passed.

Yeon-shin trained.

While the others rested, he honed the Radiant Sword Style in roadside clearings. Tested the second step of Radiant Wing Step. Ran through the Eternal Blossom Fist until his knuckles ached and the meridians in his arms hummed.

*I cannot grasp the next advancement. Not yet.*

Inspiration did not come by force. He needed to see more. Learn more.

The Desolate Sect fortress rose before them as they entered Xiangyang -- enormous, familiar, and after months on the road, strangely welcome.

*Home.*

---

The response was enormous.

From the moment he walked through the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion, seniors greeted him at every turn. Congratulations, questions, and whispered admiration followed him like a shadow.

"Lightning Flash! The achievements are unbelievable."

"Is it true about the Eight Fierce Rakshasa Sword?"

"They say he used taking-advantage tactics."

"Taking advantage? A white-rank killed a Tyrant Sword Sect elder. Would a Desolate Sect martial artist disparage that?"

"The blue robe suits you. I would give you mine if needed."

He smiled and nodded and clasped fists until the muscles in his face ached. The first fatigue since opening his middle dantian, and it came from his mouth corners.

He passed through the main training ground and entered the small hall of private rooms to train alone. He reviewed the Radiant Sword Style, tested whether more powerful force could be imbued in its flow, worked through the second footwork pattern of Radiant Wing Step.

*I need to broaden my knowledge.*

He remembered something Ma Jin had once told him. A place within the Desolate Sect fortress designed for exactly this purpose.

He turned his steps.

---

The Martial Arts Training Ground was vast.

Neatly arranged, with viewing steps along the edges and a great open floor at the centre. Unfamiliar martial artists from different squads sat scattered around the perimeter, watching. At the centre, white and blue warriors from the Seventeen Squads crossed hands -- fist techniques against sword forms, staff work against blade patterns, every combination Yeon-shin could imagine.

A training ground where all martial artists of the Divine Sword Division could freely spar. The intersection of seventeen different martial traditions.

*Instead of becoming narrow wells, meet and form an ocean.*

A middle-aged scholar with sharp features greeted him at the entrance.

"And you are?"

"Is this the Martial Arts Training Ground?"

"That is right. A youth swordsman in a blue robe..." Recognition warmed the scholar's face. "You must be Lightning Flash. I am Do Yu-won of the General Administration Office."

"Jeong Yeon-shin. Radiant Demon Wing."

They exchanged clasped-fist greetings. Do Yu-won smiled.

"I have heard much about you. All news of Desolate Sect gathers at our office. We are the ones who classify the achievements you have made." He paused. "Truly remarkable."

Yeon-shin found himself adjusting his tone. This man managed personnel within Desolate Sect. The holder of his future.

"I will tell you something in confidence," Do Yu-won said. "When you complete even one small mission, you will be promoted to blue level. Each of your achievements has been substantial. Enough points that the test will be a formality."

"Ah."

The sound came from his heart.

He was progressing.

"Congratulations in advance." Do Yu-won's smile deepened. "Anyway -- welcome here. Today's duty of managing the training ground will be worthwhile. Would you not like to see the young master's spar?"

"Young master?"

"You did not know? Right now, the heir of the Desolate Ma family is training there."

Yeon-shin followed the scholar's gesture. In the centre of the training ground, a figure moved with familiar grace -- handsome features, a face that recalled Cheong Myeong's youth. He wore a white martial uniform with the *Hwang* character of Desolate Sect.

Ma Se-in. Maternal grandfather Ma Yeon-jeok's adopted grandson.

He was winning. Consecutive victories against other white warriors, his fist techniques flowing with the Unified Blossom Fist's linear power and sudden bursts. The same martial arts Yeon-shin had studied when he created the Eternal Blossom Fist.

*He is making good use of them.*

Do Yu-won was still speaking. "There is much praise for the Desolate Ma family. Consecutive victories here at the training ground, and it has not been a month since he passed the Desolate Exam. The area around the main fortress is quite bustling."

Yeon-shin nodded.

Ma Se-in finished his bout. Looked up.

Their eyes met across the training ground.

Recognition. Challenge. Something else beneath it -- the memory of marble cracking under their feet, of a fist technique born between them, of a grandfather's trembling voice asking forgiveness.

The boy's jaw tightened.

"Also," Do Yu-won said, seemingly oblivious to the tension crackling across the hall, "there has been much talk about the Divine Sword Division lately. With the Desolate Ma family heir entering the Seventeen Squads and your own accomplishments in Radiant Demon Wing, people are saying the next generation of the Division may be the strongest in decades."

*The Divine Sword Division.*

The seventeen squads under which every martial warrior of Desolate Sect served. The road between white and blue was shorter than he had imagined. But the road from blue to black, from black to purple, from purple to the Sect Lord's chamber where the fruit of the World Tree waited --

That road was long. And the headaches at his crown were not getting better.

Ma Se-in turned away first. Walked to the edge of the training ground and sat down, arms folded.

Waiting.

Yeon-shin descended the viewing steps toward the open floor. The Desolate Sword hung at his hip. The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique's true energy circled slow and heavy through his meridians, steadied by the half-opened middle dantian.

Behind him, Do Yu-won said something to a passing administrator. The words drifted to his ears without effort.

"Record this. Lightning Flash has entered the Martial Arts Training Ground."

Across the floor, warriors from a dozen different squads looked up.

And in the training ground of Desolate Sect's Divine Sword Division, surrounded by the martial arts of seventeen traditions, Jeong Yeon-shin walked forward to meet whatever came next.
